articles.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
articles.description: Pricey Instagram handles were stolen and resold before Meta patched the exploit.
articles.content: MetaвЂ™s AI support chatbot proved unusually helpful to hackers looking to steal and resell notable Instagram accountsвЂ”the hackers simply asking the bot to change the accountsвЂ™ associated email addresses while using VPN to mask their true locations. Videos featuring the вЂњshockingly easyвЂќ exploit have been circulating among Telegram groups for hackers and security researchers, according to 404 Media . The exploit allowed hackers to take over and flip valuable Instagram accounts worth hundreds of th
articles.author: Jeremy Hsu

articles.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over alleged 'exploitation of users'
articles.description: Florida's lawsuit against OpenAI comes after a mass shooter at FSU reportedly consulted ChatGPT to plan the attack.

articles.title: This could be WindowsвЂ™ M1 moment вЂ” but expect it to cost a ton
articles.description: Nvidia's announcement that it's getting into the consumer laptop chip space with RTX Spark is huge. Apple has proved for years that Arm-based chips can perform incredibly well while also delivering great battery life - at least on the Mac. In the Windows world, performance hasn't fully matched up un
articles.content: Nvidia's announcement that it's getting into the consumer laptop chip space with RTX Spark is huge. Apple has proved for years that Arm-based chips can perform incredibly well while also delivering great battery life - at least on the Mac. In the Windows world, performance hasn't fully matched up under Qualcomm chips, mostly in the graphics department. There's clearly still untapped potential, and Nvidia seems to be promising to deliver it. This could be Windows' moment to blow us away with a ne
articles.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto

articles.title: UK banks blocked from cyber AI tool Mythos get offer from rival OpenAI
articles.description: Nine banks have been offered access to GPT 5.5 Cyber, as fierce rival Anthropic has blocked previews of its tool.

articles.title: GeminiвЂ™s new AI agent is about as good as GoogleвЂ™s demo
articles.description: Google's new "24/7" AI agent, Gemini Spark, can be shockingly good at doing things on your behalf. But I'm not sure it's worth the financial cost and potential privacy tradeoffs. The company gave me access to Spark last week. Google advertises Spark as an AI agent that can take on tasks and work on
articles.content: Google's new "24/7" AI agent, Gemini Spark, can be shockingly good at doing things on your behalf. But I'm not sure it's worth the financial cost and potential privacy tradeoffs. The company gave me access to Spark last week. Google advertises Spark as an AI agent that can take on tasks and work on them in the background - even tasks that have multiple steps - allowing you to put your phone down or walk away from your computer. It also advertises at the very top of the Spark website that it's "a
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.description: From the floor of HumanX, Ryan welcomes Songyee Yoon, managing partner at Principal Venture Partners (PVP), to chat about AI development outside the US, from the need to adapt models to local languages and culture to the challenges of the global supply-chain for things like semiconductors to how ven
articles.content: From the floor of HumanX, Ryan welcomes Songyee Yoon, managing partner at Principal Venture Partners (PVP), to chat about AI development outside the US, from the need to adapt models to local languages and culture to the challenges of the global supply-chain for things like semiconductors to how venture capital is looking at international AI companies. вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌвЂЊвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹ вЂЌ
articles.author: Phoebe Sajor

articles.content: Meta Description: Learn how to build AI agents with persistent memory using Azure AI Foundry Memory Service. articles.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
articles.description: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the companyвЂ™s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.
articles.content: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the companyвЂ™s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.
articles.author: Lucas Ropek

articles.title: Real-time relighting of Gaussian Splatting reflections on iPhone (Metal)
articles.description: I built a Metal viewer on iPhone that re-lights an already-captured 3D scene with any lighting you like. Swap or rotate the HDR environment map, and the object's reflections follow in real time, with that environment also drawn into the background. Why relighting is valuable (the practical, commerci
articles.content: I built a Metal viewer on iPhone that re-lights an already-captured 3D scene with any lighting you like. Swap or rotate the HDR environment map, and the object's reflections follow in real time, with that environment also drawn into the background. Why relighting is valuable (the practical, commercial meaning) Ordinary Gaussian Splatting has the lighting from capture time baked in. So if you place the captured object somewhere else, the highlights and shadows clash with the new surroundings and
articles.author: Daisuke Majima

articles.title: Blue Origin CEO says New Glenn will fly again before the year ends
articles.description: In a post on X, Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp provided an update on the investigation into the New Glenn launchpad explosion.
articles.content: In a post on X, Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp provided an update on the investigation into the New Glenn launchpad explosion.

articles.title: Remote work -- not AI -- has sidelined recent college graduates, research finds
articles.description: Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring.
articles.content: Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring. (Image credit: Maksym Belchenko)
articles.author: Andrea Hsu

articles.title: One by one, U.S. civil rights agency dismantles tools to fight discrimination
articles.description: The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination вЂ”against white people.
articles.content: The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination вЂ”against white people. (Image credit: Afro American Newspapers/Gado/Getty Images)

articles.content: I just needed a text box. Why was that so hard? The story of building my own simple, lightweight text editor and npm package in just 5.2 kB. articles.content: ItвЂ™s a regrettable reality that there is never enough time to cover all the interesting scientific stories we come across. So every month, we highlight a handful of the best stories that nearly slipped through the cracks. May's list includes the discovery of a possible prehistoric mining site in the Pyrenees; a new species of tiny blue octopus; why cats seem to prefer silver vine to catnip; and why political polarization might behave like a phase transition, among other noteworthy stories. articles.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
articles.description: Some report burning through their whole monthly "AI credit" allotment in a single day.
articles.content: In April, GitHub announced that it was moving subscribers from request-based billing to a usage-based model for its AI-powered Copilot service. As that new pricing model goes into effect today, many GitHub Copilot users are reporting some extreme sticker shock as they realize just how quickly their previous "normal" usage is burning through their newly limited monthly allotment of AI credits. Across social media and forums, many Copilot users are sharing personal statistics showing how just a fe
articles.author: Kyle Orland

articles.title: Moderna gets $50 million to develop mRNA Ebola vaccine against Bundibugyo
articles.description: Amid a raging Ebola outbreak, officials "urgently accelerate development" of vaccines.
articles.content: The global health organization Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI) announced Monday that it will "urgently accelerate development" of three vaccine candidates against Bundibugyo ebolavirus (BDBV), pledging a little over $60 million in the effort to extinguish an outbreak currently raging out of control in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Under the plans, CEPI has committed up to $50 million to US-based Moderna for preclinical development and Phase 1 clinical testing of it
articles.author: Beth Mole

articles.description: One day last October, sitting in the courtyard of his house in ChinaвЂ™s Henan province, Dong Hui decided to see if he could hold a pen to write.  Dong, 39, had sustained spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier that left him paralyzed from the neck down. Slowly but determinedly,
articles.content: One day last October, sitting in the courtyard of his house in ChinaвЂ™s Henan province, Dong Hui decided to see if he could hold a pen to write. Dong, 39, had sustained spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier that left him paralyzed from the neck down. Slowly but determinedly, he wrote his name, вЂњThank you,вЂќ and then the date. This was the result of an 11-month-long rehabilitation enabled by an implant in his brain. Before that process, Dong could move his arms slightly but wasnвЂ™
